summary: Stepan Company stands alone in this Chemicals cohort with 100% dormant BTP, Database Cloud, and BDC platforms — the entire data foundation required for process automation and sustainability reporting remains unbuilt, leaving no peer benchmark to follow.

Stepan Company's Ariba deployment operates as four disconnected silos despite full source-to-pay licensing — for a specialty chemicals producer dependent on volatile raw material markets, this fragmentation eliminates closed-loop procurement intelligence that competitors likely leverage.

Stepan Company's HXM architecture is collapsing as Employee Central erosion starves downstream Payroll and Talent modules — in an industry facing skilled workforce shortages, this data decay undermines retention analytics and succession planning capabilities.

As the sole Chemicals customer in this portfolio, Stepan Company's 78% consumption rate masks systemic dormancy across strategic platforms — practice leadership should treat this account as the industry baseline requiring urgent activation before Q4 renewal negotiations.

Domino's leads this CPG cohort on travel and expense maturity — Concur runs a closed-loop pattern while Kraft Heinz and S.C. Johnson show dormant or underutilized Preferred Success entitlements, leaving employee spend governance gaps unaddressed.

Supply chain fragmentation is a shared risk across all three customers — Domino's, Kraft Heinz, and S.C. Johnson each show disconnected planning-to-execution architectures, with IBP and Business Network lacking confirmed S/4 integration critical for CPG demand responsiveness.

Kraft Heinz is lagging peers on BTP platform activation despite holding the largest technology foundation investment — Integration Suite sits dormant while Domino's at least maintains steady-state integration, compounding clean-core risk for Kraft Heinz's S/4 trajectory.

S.C. Johnson trails the cohort on Data & AI activation — Business Data Cloud consumes against significant ACV but CPEA-funded AI workloads remain dormant, while Domino's has at least deployed HANA Cloud as a foundation for its contracted agentic data fabric.

Eli Lilly leads this pharma cohort on regulated training scale with SuccessFactors Learning at GxP-validated production levels, while Abbott shows no comparable learning consumption — a compliance capability gap Abbott must address for FDA audit readiness.

Abbott leads on supply chain planning activation with IBP consuming against budget, while Eli Lilly's contracted IBP signals remain dormant despite larger ACV — Abbott has operational demand sensing that Lilly has only purchased.

BTP activation is a shared critical gap across both customers — neither has operationalized the integration fabric despite significant ACV, meaning GxP-compliant automation, Joule AI agents, and regulatory workflows remain architecturally blocked for this life sciences cohort.

Eli Lilly lags Abbott on Data & AI coherence — Abbott actively consumes HANA Cloud and SAC for analytics while Lilly's data architecture shows fragmented spend management without planning activation, undermining the insight-to-action loop pharma commercialization requires.

Marathon Petroleum stands alone in this single-customer cohort with complete BTP dormancy across all solution domains — a critical gap for an energy company where real-time refinery optimization and trading analytics require active platform capabilities.

Process Intelligence failure at Marathon leaves Signavio disconnected from refinery operations — for a downstream energy player where margin optimization depends on closed-loop process mining, this represents competitive disadvantage against digitally mature peers.

Marathon's inverted source-to-pay architecture with sub-40% consumption creates margin leakage exposure — in price-sensitive refining where commodity cost control determines profitability, upstream procurement activation is an urgent operational priority.

S/4HANA advisory dormancy at Marathon signals clean-core drift risk during ERP evolution — for energy companies facing energy transition pressures and regulatory complexity, architectural guidance gaps compound transformation debt exponentially.

Border States Industries is the sole benchmark in this wholesale distribution cohort, yet its SAP journey reflects lagging status—dormant HXM modules, ungoverned BTP capacity, and imminent Concur Expense exit create compliance and operational gaps uncharacteristic of distribution leaders.

Border States' T&E architecture faces critical exposure with Concur Expense sunsetting in June—for wholesale distributors relying on field sales expense controls, this SOX risk demands immediate remediation before the ACV cliff hits.

BTP foundation weakness at Border States undermines integration between Commerce Cloud storefronts and ERP inventory—a critical gap for wholesale distributors where real-time stock visibility and B2B catalog accuracy drive customer retention.

Border States' $198K HCM investment delivers transactional HR only with Performance, Compensation, and Recruiting dormant—wholesale distributors competing for skilled warehouse and logistics talent cannot afford this talent lifecycle capability gap.

solutions_l1.name: BAIP - Data & AI
enterprise_architecture_insights: Abbott's Data & AI architecture is decapitated at the consumption layer — HANA Cloud sits as a static store with no Datasphere federation, while SAC BI consumes pipelines without planning, breaking the unified insight-to-action fabric SAP designed.

The business data fabric blueprint is contractually present but architecturally inert — without vector, multi-model, or data lake activation on HANA Cloud, Abbott has no foundation for the agentic AI and Joule analytics its SAC entitlement assumes.

Lead a Data & AI reference-architecture workshop before renewal to sequence HANA Cloud fabric activation, Datasphere federation, and SAC planning — without this, Abbott's life-sciences imperatives around R&D analytics and supply forecasting remain unreachable.

The integration chain from Data Intelligence Cloud through HANA Cloud to SAC is operational only at the endpoints — the missing semantic and AI middle tier means every new LoB use case will be rebuilt point-to-point, accumulating architectural debt.

Joule Studio agent-building and Integration Suite interface traffic share the same unfunded BTP substrate — without a unified BTP entitlement, AI extensions and integration flows compete for the same ungoverned capacity, breaking the designed separation between runtime and innovation tiers.

The architectural blueprint assumes Integration Suite mediates Joule-built extensions back to S/4 and LoB systems, but neither layer has formal capacity planning — meaning every new Joule agent silently inflates integration load with no downstream impact modeling.

Convene an EA-led BTP foundation reset workshop before any renewal motion — establish a single committed ACV envelope covering Joule Studio plus Integration Suite, with mapped downstream process dependencies, to restore commercial and architectural coherence simultaneously.

solution_architecture_insights: Joule Studio's BTP foundation is running an unfunded production workload — Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement shows 187-240% attainment with zero ACV booked, meaning extension and AI development is occurring outside any commercial envelope.

The platform is structurally underprovisioned for actual LoB demand — five consecutive months of 2x overconsumption and $21,240 cumulative overage indicate Joule Studio is the real runtime, not a sandbox, and the EA baseline must be reset.

Consumption is stabilizing but still above plan — May attainment of 187% versus January's 240% shows workload is maturing, so true-up now before the remaining seven months compound into a year-end ceiling breach.

Without a funded BTP entitlement, Joule Studio's extensibility and agent-building capabilities sit on commercially exposed infrastructure — convert the overage signal into a committed ACV tier before governance and renewal conversations harden.

solutions_l1.name: Customer Experience

Convene an EA-led Customer Experience blueprint workshop before renewal to sequence a second-storefront or B2B catalog activation tied to BTP integration, converting dormant licensed capability into the architectural value story that wins the renewal.

Johnson's entire Technology Foundation layer is contractually provisioned but architecturally inert — BTP, Integration Suite, and Joule Studio form a designed stack where each layer depends on the one below, yet none are activated, leaving no platform substrate for AI, extensions, or governed integration.

The integration backbone is being bypassed for point-to-point connections while BTP sits unused — this guarantees customisations are landing in the S/4 core, breaking the clean-core architecture and compounding upgrade debt across every downstream LoB process.

Joule Studio cannot deliver business-specific AI agents without Integration Suite exposing governed APIs and BTP hosting the runtime — the dormancy of both foundational layers makes the AI ambition architecturally unreachable, not just delayed.

Convene a single EA-led Technology Foundation activation workshop sequencing BTP enablement first, Integration Suite iFlow patterns second, and Joule agent use-cases third — without this dependency-ordered unlock, the renewal conversation has no architectural value story to defend.

solution_architecture_insights: Joule Studio's foundational runtime is dormant — Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement consumption of $83 against $1,407 budgeted shows the LoB contracted a development substrate but never stood up the AI agent and extension workloads it underwrites.
The January $77 spike followed by flatlined activity indicates an aborted pilot, not gradual ramp — without a reset, S.C. Johnson will have no Joule custom agents or skills in production by FY-end.

Architecturally, Joule Studio is the authoring layer for copilots and AI extensions across SAP — zero sustained consumption means the LoB has no pipeline of business-specific Joule use cases competing for the $1,523 unspent in Nov-Dec.
Schedule a June use-case identification sprint with the original technical sponsor to scope two Joule agent pilots, converting shelfware risk into measurable LoB automation outcomes before Q3.

solutions_l1.name: Finance and Spend Management

The dormant Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement is the single point of failure across this L1 — without BTP activation, FP&A extensions, Concur Analytics AI optimization, and Blackline reconciliation expansion all remain architecturally stranded from S/4HANA finance data.

Preferred Success Concur sits funded but unused while Concur Expense exhibits structural disruption — the customer owns the enablement vehicle to stabilize the transactional T&E backbone but has not architecturally wired it into operations.

Convene an EA-led Finance domain integration workshop before renewal to anchor one BTP-based finance scenario, expand Blackline to unenrolled S/4HANA entities, and activate Preferred Success — converting three idle entitlements into one coherent platform story.

Quarter-end automation is delivering value episodically rather than continuously — April's 101.6% attainment proves the reconciliation and task management engine works, but the LoB has not operationalized it as a repeatable monthly close playbook.

A $75.7K cumulative ACV gap indicates contracted Blackline headroom is unused — expand account reconciliation and journal entry workflows to additional S/4HANA entities to convert idle substantiation capacity into realized close-cycle value.

solutions_l1.name: Supply Chain Management
enterprise_architecture_insights: S.C. Johnson's Supply Chain Management architecture is reduced to a single live integration thread — supplier PO collaboration into S/4HANA — with the PLM-to-manufacturing bridge dormant, meaning the contracted end-to-end design-to-deliver blueprint exists only on paper.

The shared architectural failure across both L2s is broken demand-signal synchronisation — neither supplier forecast collaboration nor engineering-to-supply feedback is flowing into S/4HANA, leaving IBP planning decisions without their two contracted upstream inputs.

Without IPD operational, there is no architectural channel for new product specifications to reach Business Network suppliers, so every NPI ramp at SCJ will bypass SAP and resurface as manual data reconciliation against S/4HANA execution.

Convene an EA-led Supply Chain integration workshop anchored on one NPI-to-supplier thread spanning IPD, IBP, and Business Network — the renewal narrative depends on proving the integrated blueprint works, not on isolated module revival.

solution_architecture_insights: Supplier collaboration is architecturally narrow — only Supply Chain Collaboration is active in S.C. Johnson's Business Network, meaning PO and forecast collaboration runs without the broader sourcing or invoice automation fabric.

The flat $48,658 ceiling Jan–Apr followed by May's drop to $34,803 indicates a capped entitlement, not organic supplier onboarding — trading partner activation has stalled against contracted capacity.

Forecast collaboration with IBP and S/4HANA is the intended value driver, but the deteriorating attainment from 96% to 67.9% shows demand-signal synchronisation is not scaling across S.C. Johnson's supplier base.
Launch a targeted supplier activation wave on high-volume trading partners now to close the $36,725 cumulative gap before Q4, prioritising PO collaboration flows that feed S/4HANA execution.
